risk.desc: Potential misalignment of motor mount due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing controls and conduct alignment checks post-manufacture.

risk.desc: Cooling inlet may become obstructed if surrounding components are not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Ensure proper spacing between cooling inlet and adjacent components during assembly.

risk.desc: Service access cut may not provide sufficient clearance for tools or hands.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the size of the service access cut based on tooling requirements.

risk.desc: Insufficient evidence for fastener locking and preload methods may lead to joint failure.
risk.mit: Specify locking methods and preload values for all critical joints in the design documentation.

risk.desc: Thread engagement depth into printed material may be insufficient, leading to stripping.
risk.mit: Use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth for all threaded connections.

generated_parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
generated_parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements.value: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks
integration - role: Wing/fuselage datums, harness raceways, hatch access, connection types and load-path closure
good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled.
good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.
good: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming.
good: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface.
good: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry.

good: Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance.

good: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified.
good: One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack.
good: Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types.

good: Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints.
good: The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer.

verify.rule: Ensure all necessary sections and attributes are included.
Ensure the estimated mass matches the design requirements.

verify.value: All P0 features are mapped to geometry operations.
verify.rule: Each P0 feature must have corresponding geometry operations.

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: Interfaces should be consistent between adjacent subsystems.

verify.value: Some critical joints lack visible locking or preload evidence.
verify.rule: Critical joints should have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential mass overestimate due to conservative material assumptions.
risk.mit: Review material properties and adjust density estimates.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly difficulty if fasteners become trapped during installation.
risk.mit: Ensure assembly sequence feasibility and part reachability.

risk.desc: Inadequate strain relief for cables exiting harness raceways.
risk.mit: Add strain relief features at cable exits.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving interfaces may cause binding.
risk.mit: Specify clearances for all moving interfaces.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment due to lack of visible datum features.
risk.mit: Ensure all datum features are clearly visible and labeled.
